assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: The response mentions checking for deprecated API usage briefly in the quick fixes section but doesn't provide specific commands or detailed guidance on how to identify deprecated APIs that changed in the version upgrade

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: The response does not mention admission webhooks at all, which could be a significant source of API latency and 503 errors after an upgrade

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: The response includes sections on checking resource pressure, system pod resource consumption, and mentions increasing API server resources, addressing the need to adjust resource requirements

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: The response includes commands to check kube-system pods status with 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system' and monitoring commands to check for non-Running pods

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: The response includes the command 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_flowcontrol_rejected_requests_total' which uses the metrics endpoint to check API server performance

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: The response includes a section specifically checking network policies, CNI issues, and service mesh components like Istio with specific commands to verify their status

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: The response includes commands to check events ('kubectl get events --all-namespaces') and specifically mentions checking application readiness and pod status

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: The response does not mention HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) or VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) at all, which could be significant factors in post-upgrade performance issues
